summary: r4 fold-confirmation by the same non-authoring critic lane that issued r3 (folds applied by a separate executor; lane continuity recorded here and in phase). All r3 findings folded faithfully. L1 CLOSED: VII.2 (:193-198) entrenches Art. II, the secret floor (II.8), itself/Art. VII's entrenchment, Art. VI precedence, and Art. III's human-gate + non-authoring critic/verifier requirements, with the effect-clause closing the two-step path. FOUR fresh multi-step bypass constructions attempted — none survives invisibly: VII.1-gutting is caught by the effect-clause; repeal-and-replace is VII.1-gated and patently visible to the ratifier; stage-9 removal leaves stage-3 + ratification standing; VII.3/VII.4 weakening enables no protected weakening. All 7 advisory folds faithful and correctly cited (both NEW cites empirically verified: OMC_COEXISTENCE.md:10-16 modes table; INSTALL_MANIFEST.md:295-308 dangling-ref rule); the chapeau rewrite RESOLVES the old II.5/II.6 contradiction and corrects the CF1 cross-ref. No new defect: codex-lexeme grep clean (3 /codex/i lines :101/:117/:128, zero forbidden whole-word markers); no broken cite; no clash with unamended clauses. Secret floor now BOTH runtime-untouched AND constitutionally entrenched. Doc 211 lines. 0 blockers; 5 low/info residual advisories for the ledger.

advisories.statement: Future amendment: state that repeal/replacement/deletion of this document is itself an amendment subject to VII.2 (currently a hostile reading styles replacement as VII.1-gated 'change' outside VII.2 — unexploitable invisibly, ratifier sees it).
Future amendment: VII.2 'human-gate' (singular) should read 'human gates (stages 3 and 9)' — stage-9 removal reaches no protected weakening today but the wording should cover it.

III.4 carries a '(RATIFIED)' label while the BLOCKING-tier qualification + advisory-red-investigated sentence are r3/r4 refinements the plan gate never saw — the COMMIT-GATE record must explicitly acknowledge the tier qualification so ratification covers the refined wording.
Chapeau polish candidates ('casually'; II.3/II.4 class membership implicit) — harmless, VII.2 entrenches all of Art. II regardless.
VII.3/VII.4 remain outside VII.2's entrenchment — accepted residual (weakening them enables no protected weakening; full cycle + ratification still required).
